name: La Plana Baja y la Marjal i Estany d'Almenara — Circular desde Borriana
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alquerías Del Niño Perdido traverse a diverse landscape, ranging from coastal plains and marshlands to more elevated areas featuring passes and reservoirs. The region offers a mix of flat, scenic routes near the Mediterranean coast and more challenging paths that ascend into the hills. Road cyclists can explore historic routes and natural parks, encountering varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Alquerías Del Niño Perdido The most popular road cycling route is La Plana Baja y la Marjal i Estany d'Almenara — Circular desde Borriana, a 31.7 miles (51.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route offers a relatively flat ride through coastal plains and marshlands. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Marianet Pass – Aín Village loop from Borriana - Les Alqueries, a difficult 54.5 miles (87.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, climbing towards Marianet Pass and through Aín Village. Local road cyclists also love the Vía Augusta – Sichar Reservoir loop from Borriana - Les Alqueries, a 38.7 miles (62.3 km) trail leading through historic Vía Augusta sections and past the Sichar Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around Alquerías Del Niño Perdido is defined by coastal plains, marshlands, historic routes, and hills with reservoirs. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various abilities. The routes in Alquerías Del Niño Perdido are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alquerías Del Niño Perdido's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alquerías Del Niño Perdido?
A: There are nearly 20 road cycling routes around Alquerías Del Niño Perdido, offering a diverse range of experiences from flat coastal rides to challenging climbs into the hills. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options for easy, moderate, and difficult rides.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in the area?
A: Yes, Alquerías Del Niño Perdido offers several easy road cycling routes. A great option for beginners is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883216306/la-plana-baja-y-la-marjal-i-estany-d-almenara-circular-desde-borriana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Plana Baja y la Marjal i Estany d'Almenara — Circular desde Borriana&lt;/a&gt;, which is relatively flat and takes you through coastal plains and marshlands.

Q: What kind of challenging road cycling routes can I find around Alquerías Del Niño Perdido?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region features routes with significant elevation gain. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marianet Pass – Aín Village loop from Borriana - Les Alqueries&lt;/a&gt;, which climbs towards Marianet Pass and through the scenic Aín Village. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Santa Quitería – Vía Augusta loop from Borriana - Les Alqueries&lt;/a&gt;, offering a long ride with substantial ascents.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Alquerías Del Niño Perdido?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883216306/la-plana-baja-y-la-marjal-i-estany-d-almenara-circular-desde-borriana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Plana Baja y la Marjal i Estany d'Almenara — Circular desde Borriana&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Augusta – Sichar Reservoir loop from Borriana - Les Alqueries&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert de les Palmes Pass – Acequia Travesera loop from Borriana - Les Alqueries&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by beautiful natural and historical sites. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Aragonesas&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3247125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de la Font de Cabres&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Mijares River&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Augusta – Sichar Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you past the picturesque Sichar Reservoir.

Q: What historical landmarks can be explored while road cycling in the region?
A: The area is rich in history. You can cycle along sections of the historic Vía Augusta, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5971029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Quiteria and Medieval Bridge of Almassora&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5174637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrelló del Boverot archaeological site&lt;/a&gt; is another point of interest near some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking around Alquerías Del Niño Perdido?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for road cycling in Alquerías Del Niño Perdido. During these seasons, temperatures are pleasant, and the weather is generally stable, offering comfortable conditions for longer rides. Summer can be very hot, especially inland.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Alquerías Del Niño Perdido?
A: The road cycling routes in Alquerías Del Niño Perdido are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from flat coastal paths to challenging climbs, and the opportunity to explore both natural landscapes and historical sites.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through marshlands or coastal areas?
A: Yes, the region's diverse landscape includes coastal plains and marshlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883216306/la-plana-baja-y-la-marjal-i-estany-d-almenara-circular-desde-borriana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Plana Baja y la Marjal i Estany d'Almenara — Circular desde Borriana&lt;/a&gt; route is a prime example, offering a ride through these unique ecosystems.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Alquerías Del Niño Perdido?
A: Road cycling around Alquerías Del Niño Perdido offers a mix of terrain. You'll find flat routes along the coastal plains and through marshlands, as well as more elevated areas featuring passes and hills with reservoirs. This variety ensures options for all preferences, from leisurely rides to strenuous climbs.

Q: Are there any routes that include reservoirs or bodies of water?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Augusta – Sichar Reservoir loop from Borriana - Les Alqueries&lt;/a&gt; is a notable route that takes you past the beautiful Sichar Reservoir, offering scenic views along the way.
tips: It is a protected space within the Natura 2000 network, a flower and fauna micro-reserve and contains freshwater lagoons. It is a very quiet and picturesque place next to the coast of the Plana Baja.
